page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring 8 shared vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon Cascadelake cores running at 2.0 GHz. The system utilizes the x86_64 architecture and includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 350 GB local NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks place this server primarily in the average tier for its hardware class. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1437.59, and the multi-core score is 11603.93. Memory bandwidth tests show 831.82 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and 62.89 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. The server handles static web serving at a throughput of 8.38 GB/sec and processes Redis SET operations at 12.74 million operations per second.

A notable performance tradeoff is observed in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, which scored 35.65 GB/sec, placing it in the weak category. Additionally, LLM inference on a medium 7B model is poor, yielding 11.11 tokens per second for prompt processing and 5.01 tokens per second for text generation. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose web serving, database operations, and lightweight application hosting where dedicated CPU allocation is not strictly required.
description: The Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with 8 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes a 350 GB local NVMe SSD alongside one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most standard operations, including a stress-ng multi-core score of 11603.93 and a static web serving throughput of 8.38 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference tasks show weaker performance. Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads like web serving, database operations, and basic application hosting that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel is a High Performance server with 8 shared Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 350 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced mid-tier performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-intel features 8 shared Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 350 GB NVMe storage, suitable for web serving and database tasks.
tagline: An 8-core Intel Xeon virtual server with local NVMe storage, delivering balanced mid-range performance for standard web and database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake cores running at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in database operations and static web serving
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and LLM inference
Best suited for web serving, database operations, and general-purpose hosting
